obj.title: Talk on jobs in fashion design

sentence.content: Hugo: Hi Chantal. What did you think of the talk, then?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo：嗨，Chantal。那你觉得这次座谈怎么样？

sentence.content: Chantal: Hi Hugo. I thought it was good once I’d moved seats.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：你好，Hugo。我想，一旦我换了座位，它就会很好。

sentence.content: Hugo: Oh – were the people beside you chatting or something?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo： 哦，你旁边的人是在聊天还是什么？

sentence.content: Chantal: It wasn’t that. I went early so that I’d get a seat and not have to stand, but then this guy sat right in front of me and he was so tall!
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：不是的。我去得很早，这样我就能找到一个座位，不用站着。但后来有个人就坐在我前面，他很高！

sentence.content: Hugo: It’s hard to see through people’s heads, isn’t it?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo：要越过别人的头顶看过去很难，不是吗？

sentence.content: Chantal: Impossible! Anyway, to answer your question, I thought it was really interesting, especially what the speaker said about the job market.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：不可能。总之，回答你的问题，我认为这真的很有趣，尤其是演讲者对就业市场的看法。

sentence.content: Hugo: Me too. I mean we know we’re going into a really competitive field so it’s obvious that we may struggle to get work.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo： 我也是。我的意思是，我们知道我们要进入一个竞争非常激烈的领域，所以很明显我们可能很难找到工作。

sentence.content: Chantal: That’s right – and we know we can’t all have that ‘dream job’.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：是的，我们知道我们不可能都有 "梦想的工作"。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah, but it looks like there’s a whole range of … areas of work that we hadn’t even thought of – like fashion journalism, for instance.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo：是的，但是看起来有一系列的......我们甚至没有想到的工作领域--比如说时尚新闻。

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah – I wasn’t expecting so many career options.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：是的--我没想到会有这么多职业选择。

sentence.content: Hugo: Mmm. Overall, she had quite a strong message, didn’t she?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo： 嗯。总的来说，她传递了相当震撼的信息，不是吗？

sentence.content: Chantal: She did. She kept saying things like ‘I know you all think this, but …’ and then she’d tell us how it really is.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的。她一直在说 "我知道你们都这么想，但是......"，然后她会告诉我们事情的真实情况。

sentence.content: Hugo: Perhaps she thinks students are a bit narrow-minded about the industry.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 也许她认为学生对这个行业有点狭隘的看法。

sentence.content: Chantal: It was a bit harsh, though! We know it’s a tough industry.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 不过，她有点刻薄！我们知道这是一个艰难的行业。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah – and we’re only first years, after all. We’ve got a lot to learn.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 是的，而且我们只是一年级学生，还有很多要学习的。

sentence.content: Chantal: Exactly. Do you think our secondary-school education should have been more career-focused?
sentence.content_zh: Chantal:是呀。你觉得我们中学的教育应该更加注重职业发展吗？

sentence.content: Hugo: Well, we had numerous talks on careers, which was good, but none of them were very inspiring. They could have asked more people like today’s speaker to talk to us.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 嗯，我们确实有很多关于职业的讲座，这是好的，但是没有一个非常激励人心。他们本可以请更多像今天的演讲者这样的专家来和我们交流。

sentence.content: Chantal: I agree. We were told about lots of different careers – just when we needed to be, but not by the experts who really know stuff.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 我同意。我们被告知了很多不同的职业，只是在我们需要的时候，但并不是由真正了解这些行业的专家来告诉我们。

sentence.content: Hugo: So did today’s talk influence your thoughts on what career you’d like to take up in the future?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo:那么，今天的讲座是否对你将来选择职业有影响？

sentence.content: Chantal: Well, I promised myself that I’d go through this course and keep an open mind till the end.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 嗯，我答应自己要完成这门课程，并持开放的心态到最后。

sentence.content: Hugo: But I think it’s better to pick an area of the industry now and then aim to get better and better at it.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 但我认为最好是现在选择一个行业领域，并努力变得越来越好。

sentence.content: Chantal: Well, I think we’ll just have to differ on that issue!
sentence.content_zh: Chantal:嗯，我想在这个问题上我们的观点可能会有所不同！

sentence.content: Hugo: One thing’s for certain, though. From what she said, we’ll be unpaid assistants in the industry for quite a long time.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 但有一点是确定的。根据她的说法，我们在这个行业里将会做很长时间的无薪助理工作。

sentence.content: Chantal: Mmm.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 嗯。

sentence.content: Hugo: I’m prepared for that, aren’t you?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 我做好了准备，你呢？

sentence.content: Chantal: Actually, I’m not going to accept that view.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 实际上，我不会接受那种观点。

sentence.content: Hugo: Really? But she knows it’s the case – and everyone else says the same.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo:真的吗？但她知道这是事实，而且其他人也都这么说。

sentence.content: Chantal: That doesn’t mean it has to be true for me.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 那并不意味着对我来说也是真的。

sentence.content: Hugo: OK. Well – I hope you’re right!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 好吧，希望你是对的！

sentence.content: Chantal: I thought the speaker’s account of her first job was fascinating.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 我觉得演讲者讲述她的第一份工作很有趣。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah – she admitted she was lucky to get work being a personal dresser for a musician. She didn’t even apply for the job and there she was getting paid to choose all his clothes.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 是的 - 她承认自己能得到一个音乐家的私人服装师的工作是很幸运的。她甚至没有申请这份工作，但她却因此而得到了报酬。

sentence.content: Chantal: It must have felt amazing – though she said all she was looking for back then was experience, not financial reward.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 那肯定感觉很棒 - 尽管她说当时她只是在追求经验，而不是财务回报。

sentence.content: Hugo: Mmm. And then he was so mean, telling her she was more interested in her own appearance than his!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 嗯。然后他却很刻薄地告诉她，她更关注自己的外表而不是他的！

sentence.content: Chantal: But – she did realise he was right about that, which really made me think. I’m always considering my own clothes but now I can see you should be focusing on your client!
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 但是 - 她确实意识到他在这方面是对的，这让我真的思考了很多。我总是考虑自己的衣服，但现在我明白应该更关注客户！

sentence.content: Hugo: She obviously regretted losing the job.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 显然她后悔失去了那份工作。

sentence.content: Chantal: Well, as she said, she should have hidden her negative feelings about him, but she didn’t.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 嗯，正如她所说的，她本应该隐藏自己对他的负面情绪，但她没有做到。

sentence.content: Hugo: It was really brave the way she picked herself up and took that job in retail. Fancy working in a shop after that!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo:她振作起来并在零售业找到那份工作的方式真的很勇敢。在那之后去一家商店工作，真是有点奇怪！

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah – well, she recommended we all do it at some point. I guess as a designer you’d get to find out some useful information, like how big or small the average shopper is.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的 - 她建议我们都应该有这样的经历。我想作为设计师，你可以了解到一些有用的信息，比如一般购物者的体型大小。

sentence.content: Hugo: I think that’s an issue for manufacturers, not designers. However, it would be useful to know if there’s a gap in the market – you know, an item that no one’s stocking but that consumers are looking for.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 我认为那是制造商的问题，而不是设计师的问题。不过，知道市场上是否存在空白 - 也就是说，没有人销售但消费者需要的物品 - 这对我们来说是有用的。

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah, people don’t give up searching. They also take things back to the store if they aren’t right.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的，人们不会放弃寻找。如果他们买到的东西不合适，他们也会把它们退回商店。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah. Imagine you worked in an expensive shop and you found out the garments sold there were being returned because they … fell apart in the wash!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 是的。想象一下，你在一家昂贵的商店工作，然后发现那里销售的衣服在洗涤过程中竟然散架了！

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah, it would be good to know that kind of thing.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的，了解那种情况会很有用。

TWO
letters,
A-E
.

obj.content: Which [b]TWO[/b]  mistakes did the speaker admit she made in her first job?
obj.html_content: Which TWO mistakes did the speaker admit she made in her first job?

obj.content: being dishonest to her employer
obj.html_content: being dishonest to her employer

obj.content: paying too much attention to how she looked
obj.html_content: paying too much attention to how she looked

obj.content: expecting to become well known
obj.html_content: expecting to become well known

obj.content: trying to earn a lot of money
obj.html_content: trying to earn a lot of money

obj.content: openly disliking her client
obj.html_content: openly disliking her client

obj.content: Which [b]TWO[/b] pieces of retail information do Hugo and Chantal agree would be useful?
obj.html_content: Which TWO pieces of retail information do Hugo and Chantal agree would be useful?

obj.content: the reasons people return fashion items
obj.html_content: the reasons people return fashion items

obj.content: how much time people have to shop for clothes
obj.html_content: how much time people have to shop for clothes

obj.content: fashion designs people want but can't find
obj.html_content: fashion designs people want but can't find

obj.content: the best time of year for fashion buying
obj.html_content: the best time of year for fashion buying

obj.content: the most popular fashion sizes
obj.html_content: the most popular fashion sizes

sentence.content_zh: Hugo： 哦，你旁边的人是在聊天还是什么？

sentence.content: Chantal: It wasn’t that. I went early so that I’d get a seat and not have to stand, but then this guy sat right in front of me and he was so tall!
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：不是的。我去得很早，这样我就能找到一个座位，不用站着。但后来有个人就坐在我前面，他很高！

sentence.content: Hugo: It’s hard to see through people’s heads, isn’t it?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo：要越过别人的头顶看过去很难，不是吗？

sentence.content: Chantal: Impossible! Anyway, to answer your question, I thought it was really interesting, especially what the speaker said about the job market.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：不可能。总之，回答你的问题，我认为这真的很有趣，尤其是演讲者对就业市场的看法。

sentence.content: Hugo: Me too. I mean we know we’re going into a really competitive field so it’s obvious that we may struggle to get work.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo： 我也是。我的意思是，我们知道我们要进入一个竞争非常激烈的领域，所以很明显我们可能很难找到工作。

sentence.content: Chantal: That’s right – and we know we can’t all have that ‘dream job’.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：是的，我们知道我们不可能都有 "梦想的工作"。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah, but it looks like there’s a whole range of … areas of work that we hadn’t even thought of – like fashion journalism, for instance.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo：是的，但是看起来有一系列的......我们甚至没有想到的工作领域--比如说时尚新闻。

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah – I wasn’t expecting so many career options.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal：是的--我没想到会有这么多职业选择。

sentence.content: Hugo: Mmm. Overall, she had quite a strong message, didn’t she?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo： 嗯。总的来说，她传递了相当震撼的信息，不是吗？

sentence.content: Chantal: She did. She kept saying things like ‘I know you all think this, but …’ and then she’d tell us how it really is.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的。她一直在说 "我知道你们都这么想，但是......"，然后她会告诉我们事情的真实情况。

sentence.content: Hugo: Perhaps she thinks students are a bit narrow-minded about the industry.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 也许她认为学生对这个行业有点狭隘的看法。

sentence.content: Chantal: It was a bit harsh, though! We know it’s a tough industry.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 不过，她有点刻薄！我们知道这是一个艰难的行业。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah – and we’re only first years, after all. We’ve got a lot to learn.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 是的，而且我们只是一年级学生，还有很多要学习的。

sentence.content: Chantal: Exactly. Do you think our secondary-school education should have been more career-focused?
sentence.content_zh: Chantal:是呀。你觉得我们中学的教育应该更加注重职业发展吗？

sentence.content: Hugo: Well, we had numerous talks on careers, which was good, but none of them were very inspiring. They could have asked more people like today’s speaker to talk to us.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 嗯，我们确实有很多关于职业的讲座，这是好的，但是没有一个非常激励人心。他们本可以请更多像今天的演讲者这样的专家来和我们交流。

sentence.content: Chantal: I agree. We were told about lots of different careers – just when we needed to be, but not by the experts who really know stuff.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 我同意。我们被告知了很多不同的职业，只是在我们需要的时候，但并不是由真正了解这些行业的专家来告诉我们。

sentence.content: Hugo: So did today’s talk influence your thoughts on what career you’d like to take up in the future?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo:那么，今天的讲座是否对你将来选择职业有影响？

sentence.content: Chantal: Well, I promised myself that I’d go through this course and keep an open mind till the end.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 嗯，我答应自己要完成这门课程，并持开放的心态到最后。

sentence.content: Hugo: But I think it’s better to pick an area of the industry now and then aim to get better and better at it.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 但我认为最好是现在选择一个行业领域，并努力变得越来越好。

sentence.content: Chantal: Well, I think we’ll just have to differ on that issue!
sentence.content_zh: Chantal:嗯，我想在这个问题上我们的观点可能会有所不同！

sentence.content: Hugo: One thing’s for certain, though. From what she said, we’ll be unpaid assistants in the industry for quite a long time.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 但有一点是确定的。根据她的说法，我们在这个行业里将会做很长时间的无薪助理工作。

sentence.content: Chantal: Mmm.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 嗯。

sentence.content: Hugo: I’m prepared for that, aren’t you?
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 我做好了准备，你呢？

sentence.content: Chantal: Actually, I’m not going to accept that view.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 实际上，我不会接受那种观点。

sentence.content: Hugo: Really? But she knows it’s the case – and everyone else says the same.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo:真的吗？但她知道这是事实，而且其他人也都这么说。

sentence.content: Chantal: That doesn’t mean it has to be true for me.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 那并不意味着对我来说也是真的。

sentence.content: Hugo: OK. Well – I hope you’re right!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 好吧，希望你是对的！

sentence.content: Chantal: I thought the speaker’s account of her first job was fascinating.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 我觉得演讲者讲述她的第一份工作很有趣。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah – she admitted she was lucky to get work being a personal dresser for a musician. She didn’t even apply for the job and there she was getting paid to choose all his clothes.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 是的 - 她承认自己能得到一个音乐家的私人服装师的工作是很幸运的。她甚至没有申请这份工作，但她却因此而得到了报酬。

sentence.content: Chantal: It must have felt amazing – though she said all she was looking for back then was experience, not financial reward.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 那肯定感觉很棒 - 尽管她说当时她只是在追求经验，而不是财务回报。

sentence.content: Hugo: Mmm. And then he was so mean, telling her she was more interested in her own appearance than his!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 嗯。然后他却很刻薄地告诉她，她更关注自己的外表而不是他的！

sentence.content: Chantal: But – she did realise he was right about that, which really made me think. I’m always considering my own clothes but now I can see you should be focusing on your client!
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 但是 - 她确实意识到他在这方面是对的，这让我真的思考了很多。我总是考虑自己的衣服，但现在我明白应该更关注客户！

sentence.content: Hugo: She obviously regretted losing the job.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 显然她后悔失去了那份工作。

sentence.content: Chantal: Well, as she said, she should have hidden her negative feelings about him, but she didn’t.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 嗯，正如她所说的，她本应该隐藏自己对他的负面情绪，但她没有做到。

sentence.content: Hugo: It was really brave the way she picked herself up and took that job in retail. Fancy working in a shop after that!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo:她振作起来并在零售业找到那份工作的方式真的很勇敢。在那之后去一家商店工作，真是有点奇怪！

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah – well, she recommended we all do it at some point. I guess as a designer you’d get to find out some useful information, like how big or small the average shopper is.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的 - 她建议我们都应该有这样的经历。我想作为设计师，你可以了解到一些有用的信息，比如一般购物者的体型大小。

sentence.content: Hugo: I think that’s an issue for manufacturers, not designers. However, it would be useful to know if there’s a gap in the market – you know, an item that no one’s stocking but that consumers are looking for.
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 我认为那是制造商的问题，而不是设计师的问题。不过，知道市场上是否存在空白 - 也就是说，没有人销售但消费者需要的物品 - 这对我们来说是有用的。

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah, people don’t give up searching. They also take things back to the store if they aren’t right.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的，人们不会放弃寻找。如果他们买到的东西不合适，他们也会把它们退回商店。

sentence.content: Hugo: Yeah. Imagine you worked in an expensive shop and you found out the garments sold there were being returned because they … fell apart in the wash!
sentence.content_zh: Hugo: 是的。想象一下，你在一家昂贵的商店工作，然后发现那里销售的衣服在洗涤过程中竟然散架了！

sentence.content: Chantal: Yeah, it would be good to know that kind of thing.
sentence.content_zh: Chantal: 是的，了解那种情况会很有用。

sectionSheet.title: Talk on jobs in fashion design

officialExplain.explain: 本题需要选择女生Chantal在演讲开始时遇到的问题。首先Chantal提到换座位之后就好多了，引出男生Tim提问是否是旁边人在聊天影响了听演讲，对应C选项，但立即被女生否定。之后女生提及到得早有座位，否定B选项。but转折词后引出真正的原因，即前面坐的男生太高，预判A选项。接下来Tim的回应It’s hard to see through people’s heads以及女生的回答Impossible也可进一步验证A选项。

本题需选出两人对就业市场的什么情况感到惊讶，即需要选出说话者之前没有预料到的事情。题干中的job market原词重现。随后男生先提到A选项的competitive，但其中we know表示在意料中，否定A选项。后面得到女生肯定后，男生通过转折词but引出B选项内容，variety替换原文的a whole range of，之后提到we hadn’t even thought of，即之前没有想过，女生随后也说I wasn’t expecting so many career options，对男生的观点表示赞同。so many options同样是对B选项中variety的替换。C选项未提及。

本题需要选出两人对主讲人传达的信息一致认同的方面。根据a strong message可定位。原文谈到主讲人认为学生们比较narrow-minded，即思想狭隘，对行业认知有误解，可以联想到C选项的critical，但C选项所说的是对行业的批判，而非对学生认知的批判。随后出现女生对主讲人的评价a bit harsh，男生立刻表示赞同，并通过after all补充说明赞同的理由，对应A选项，即二人觉得主讲人对学生的评价有失公允。

原文听到secondary-school和career-focused时即可定位该题。男生首先提到numerous talks on careers, which was good，可否定B选项。后文转折词but后引出男生觉得不太满意的地方，could have asked表示过去本可以做却没有做，即男生希望中学的就业指导可以找今天这样的主讲人来做演讲，可初步确定C选项。随后女生表示赞同，并提到just when we needed to be，否定A选项，后续but后提到了不满的方面，即演讲的人并不是懂行的专家，即可最终确定C选项。

题干中的future原词重现，男生提问听这个演讲是否会影响对将来工作的想法。女生首先回答说keep an open mind till the end，即不想现在就做决定。男生的回答首先使用了转折词but，随后I think it’s better…表明自己的态度，即现在就选定职业方向，并致力于在这个方向越做越好。后续女生回答differ on that issue，表示不赞同男生说法。两人有分歧的是何时决定职业方向，对应选项B。
本题需要选出男生Hugo的看法。题干中的unpaid assistant原词重现，随后男生说到I’m prepared for that，即做好了准备，对应A选项的realistic，也就是接受这个现实。

本题需要选出主讲人承认自己的第一份工作所犯的错误。原文admit原词重现，但首先提到了第一份工作幸运的方面，并不是要听取的信息。接着女生谈到主讲人当时追求experience而非financial reward，否定了D选项。随后男生提到musician不友好地指出主讲人more interested in her own appearance than his，对应B选项。后文通过she did realise he was right about that表示speaker后来也发觉musician指出的问题确实存在，可确定B选项为正确选项之一。接下来的对话中提到she obviously regretted losing the job, 后文提到she should have hidden her negative feelings about him，即对regret进行了补充说明，对应E选项openly disliking her client。should have done意为过去本应做却未做，表示说话者对过去行为的后悔、内疚，即对应题目中承认错误，因此可确定E选项为另一个正确选项。

题干中的retail原词重现，需要选出二人都认为有用的信息。useful一词先由女生说出，随后提到how big or small the average shopper is，对应E选项的size。但随后男生说他认为这是制造商的问题，而不是设计师的问题，否定E选项。however后男生提出了自己认为有用的方面，即了解gap in the market，并进行补充说明an item no one’s stocking but that consumers are looking for, 随后女生表示赞同，可确定C选项为正确选项之一，选项中的people即原文中的consumers。女生随后用also引出另一个方面，即take things back to the store，对应A选项的return，男生随后也表示赞同，并补充说明了退货的原因。随后女生通过Yeah, it would be good to know…再次表达赞同，即可确定A选项为另一个正确选项。
